checks.label: Workspace writable
checks.recommendation: Fix workspace permissions before launching unattended.

checks.label: Workspace path resolved
checks.recommendation: Open a real workspace folder.

checks.label: Autonomous transport is codexCli
checks.recommendation: Use codexLoop.transport=codexCli for unattended builds.

checks.label: Codex CLI responds
checks.recommendation: Install or expose Codex CLI, or set codexLoop.codexExecutable.

checks.label: Codex exec command shape responds
checks.evidence: exit=0; stdout=Run Codex non-interactively Usage: codex exec [OPTIONS] [PROMPT] codex exec [OPTIONS] <COMMAND> [ARGS] Commands: resume Resume a previous session by id or pick ; stderr=
checks.recommendation: Update codexLoop.codexExtraArgs/codexExecutable if codex exec flags changed.

checks.label: Approval policy is unattended
checks.recommendation: Set codexLoop.codexApprovalPolicy=never for overnight runs.

checks.label: Sandbox can write workspace
checks.recommendation: Use workspace-write or danger-full-access for builders.

checks.label: Task timeout supports long generations
checks.recommendation: Use at least 600000ms for large factory tasks.

checks.label: Retries are available
checks.recommendation: Use maxRetries >= 3.

checks.label: Research has web search
checks.recommendation: Use cached/live search for Product Hunt/GitHub research phases.

checks.label: No-git workspace is tolerated
checks.recommendation: Optional: initialize git for safer diffs/worktrees.

checks.label: Factory mode enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable factory mode for autonomous planning.

checks.label: Preset is recognized
checks.recommendation: Choose a supported factory preset.

checks.label: Task count is large enough
checks.recommendation: Use 25-80 tasks for serious MVP/portfolio builds.

checks.label: Strict quality profile
checks.recommendation: Use strict for overnight autonomous work.

checks.label: Verify after each task
checks.recommendation: Keep verification enabled to avoid false completion.

checks.label: Automatic repair enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable repairOnVerificationFailure.

checks.label: Final review enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable reviewAtEnd.

checks.label: Supervisor decision guard enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable the supervisor guard.

checks.label: Adaptive planning enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable adaptive planning so new tasks can be created.

checks.label: Autonomy budget allows long run
checks.recommendation: Use forever mode or at least 3-4 hours.

checks.label: GitHub Scout enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable GitHub Scout for frontier repairs.

checks.label: Creative Studio enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable Creative Studio for image/deck/landing assets.

checks.label: Browser QA frontier enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable browser QA for real navigation/render checks.

checks.label: Safe-coding review enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable safe-coding/privacy review.

checks.label: Release readiness enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable release readiness review.

checks.label: Prompt safe-mode enabled
checks.recommendation: Enable policySafeModeEnabled to reduce provider false positives.

checks.label: Pause on unresolved failed verification
checks.recommendation: Enable pauseOnFailedVerification so the factory does not build on a red suite.

checks.label: Repair depth handles transient red tests
checks.recommendation: Use at least 2 repair attempts for real projects.

checks.label: Workspace prompt-risk wording scan
checks.recommendation: Reframe prompts/tasks toward safe-coding/privacy language.

checks.label: Portfolio output root is safe
checks.recommendation: Use a dedicated folder such as products, not src/pages/.codex-loop/node_modules/dist.

checks.label: Project registry exists and parses
checks.recommendation: Generate .codex-loop/factory/project-registry.json during portfolio synthesis.

checks.label: Registry product count matches portfolio target
checks.recommendation: Each product needs a unique registry entry with slug, title, path, status and verification.

checks.label: No duplicate or unsafe project paths
checks.recommendation: Fix project-registry slugs/paths before building more products.

checks.label: Portfolio does not mix product apps into workspace root
checks.recommendation: Keep product-specific code under products/<slug>/ and use root only for orchestration/shared docs.

checks.label: Output/topologia
checks.recommendation: Keep product apps under products/<slug>/ and maintain .codex-loop/factory/project-registry.json.

checks.label: Runtime/toolchain
checks.evidence: codex=true; execHelp=0; node=true; npm=true; rg=false; rgShim=true; timeout=900000; retries=3
checks.recommendation: Expose Codex/Node/npm on PATH, verify codex exec --help, keep local tool shims ready, and use long timeouts plus at least 3 retries.

checks.label: Estado/locks
checks.recommendation: Use run.lock, stale detection and unfinished-run detection before starting another factory.

checks.label: Calidad real
checks.recommendation: Add build/test/coverage scripts and repair until coverage evidence approaches 80%.

checks.label: Render/browser
checks.recommendation: Frontend products need a real navigation/render smoke path, not only build.

checks.label: Packaging
checks.recommendation: Only claim a desktop/binary artifact when the packaging config/script or actual artifact exists.

checks.label: Secretos/datos
checks.recommendation: Use placeholders/env.example only; block generated credential-shaped values in changed files.

checks.label: Licencias/GitHub Scout
checks.recommendation: Use GitHub for patterns first; adapt only permissive compatible code and record attribution.

checks.label: Research trust
checks.recommendation: Treat market/web findings as noisy; store source signals, confidence and caveats.

checks.label: Coste/tiempo
checks.recommendation: Forever mode needs a supervisor guard; finite mode needs an explicit budget or task cap.

checks.label: Naming
checks.recommendation: Use stable kebab-case product slugs and avoid third-party brands in product identity.

checks.label: Rollback
checks.recommendation: Keep snapshots or Git available before autonomous workers edit product files.

checks.label: Final truth gate
checks.recommendation: Do not declare done until build/test/render/packaging/data/license evidence is present or residual risk is explicit.

codexExecHelp.stdout:
Run Codex non-interactively

Usage: codex exec [OPTIONS] [PROMPT]
       codex exec [OPTIONS] <COMMAND> [ARGS]

Commands:
  resume  Resume a previous session by id or pick the most recent with --last
  review  Run a code review against the current repository
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Arguments:
  [PROMPT]
          Initial instructions for the agent. If not provided as an argument (or if `-` is used),
          instructions are read from stdin. If stdin is piped and a prompt is also provided, stdin
          is appended as a `<stdin>` block

Options:
  -c, --config <key=value>
          Override a configuration value that would otherwise be loaded from `~/.codex/config.toml`.
          Use a dotted path (`foo.bar.baz`) to override nested values. The `value` portion is parsed
          as TOML. If it fails to parse as TOML, the raw string is used as a literal.
          
          Examples: - `-c model="o3"` - `-c 'sandbox_permissions=["disk-full-read-access"]'` - `-c
          shell_environment_policy.inherit=all`

      --enable <FEATURE>
          Enable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=true`

      --disable <FEATURE>
          Disable a feature (repeatable). Equivalent to `-c features.<name>=false`

  -i, --image <FILE>...
          Optional image(s) to attach to the initial prompt

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Model the agent should use

      --oss
          Use open-source provider

      --local-provider <OSS_PROVIDER>
          Specify which local provider to use (lmstudio or ollama). If not specified with --oss,
          will use config default or show selection

  -p, --profile <CONFIG_PROFILE>
          Configuration profile from config.toml to specify default options

  -s, --sandbox <SANDBOX_MODE>
          Select the sandbox policy to use when executing model-generated shell commands
          
          [possible values: read-only, workspace-write, danger-full-access]

      --da
